changes.reason: STALE_0DTE breakout fallback is the weakest routed slice by efficiency: 2 trades, 40.5 PnL/exposure hour, 52.6m median hold, and -250.25 worst MAE. Raising slope from 0.08 to 0.10 keeps this as a momentum-only ES fallback instead of a stale-data drift entry.

The no-0DTE breakout fallback should require clean ES structure because it lacks live 0DTE confirmation. Moving from 4 to 5 is a narrow fallback-only quality test and does not alter VALID_0DTE profiles.

Current stale breakout fallback uses a 6-point dynamic cap while producing low PnL/hour and high MAE. Reducing to 4 should shorten stale fallback exposure and test whether this profile is profitable only when it exits faster.

Pullback no-0DTE fallback is positive but low efficiency at 83.2 PnL/exposure hour. Tightening structure from 4 to 5 tests whether stale fallback trades need stronger ES confirmation without touching the main VALID_0DTE pullback edge.

expected_effect: Should improve fallback exposure quality, reduce stale-state MAE/hold drag, and leave VALID_0DTE performance unchanged. Tradeoff is possible loss of a few positive stale fallback trades; reject if total net profit falls below 98% of run_001 or fallback trade count simply starves without PnL/exposure improvement.
label: no-0dte-fallback-quality
rationale: Iteration 2 should follow Batch G. The analyzer has option data-state slices, and the weak spot is not missing 0DTE itself but stale/expired fallback efficiency. This is one logical family using only routed no-0DTE profile keys, avoiding option-dependent profiles and avoiding global veto tightening. The sample is small, so this is a narrow risk-adjusted probe rather than a broad deletion filter.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: STALE_0DTE produced only $162.8 over 5 trades. breakout_long_no_0dte stale trades had 52.6m median hold, -210.6 average MAE, -250.25 worst MAE, and only 40.5 PnL/exposure hour, while VALID_0DTE contributed $5450.6 and should be left untouched.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Stale 0DTE Momentum-Only Fallback
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Only 5 stale trades exist, so tightening may overfit and remove positive fallback PnL without improving full-sample objective.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: 0DTE state is STALE_0DTE or EXPIRED_0DTE and routing selects no-0DTE ES fallback profiles.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Fallback PnL/exposure hour rises above 150, fallback average MAE improves, VALID_0DTE PnL is unchanged, and total net profit remains at least 98% of run_001.

strategy_plan_reference.market_regime_read: The current leader is a high-win, long-only SPX continuation/pullback system over a short 12.3 calendar-day window. Most profit comes from VALID_0DTE and VALID_1DTE_ONLY states,...

new_strategy_ideas.name: MAE-Aware Recovery Exit Overlay
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Worst MAE and exposure hours fall materially while net profit remains at least 95-98% of baseline and PnL/exposure hour improves.

new_strategy_ideas.name: High-ADX Call Wall Acceptance Expansion
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 5-10 additional trades with PnL/exposure hour above 250, avg MAE below baseline, and no degradation in pullback_long fills.

new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Test
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: VALID_1DTE_ONLY PnL rises without worsening worst MAE beyond -400 or reducing PnL/exposure hour below breakout_long_1dte baseline.

new_strategy_ideas.name: Delta Charm Breakout Activation Probe
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Incremental trades show PnL/exposure hour above 225, median hold below 15m, and worst MAE below current system worst.
